context.surface: Workspace onboarding checklist inside the existing dashboard shell
context.design_goal: Help a new admin finish the first critical setup steps without feeling lost or overcommitted.
constraints: Keep the concept inside the current dashboard shell instead of creating a full-screen wizard.
Stay low fidelity and approval oriented rather than implementation ready.
non_goals: No editable prototype or preview editor scope.
communication_focus: What happens if I start a setup step now?

machine_readable.problem_frame: New workspace admins land on a sparse dashboard and do not know which setup step matters first, which creates hesitation and setup abandonment.
top_user_questions: What is the first thing I should do?
Which setup steps are required versus optional?
What will happen if I start this step now?
machine_readable.human_explanation: Start with the first required setup step below. We will show what each step does before you commit, and you can defer optional tasks until later.

recommendations.summary: Anchor the first required setup action above the fold.
recommendations.rationale: A single clear next step reduces scanning and decision fatigue during first-run setup.
recommendations.expected_user_outcome: Admins can begin setup immediately without exploring unrelated dashboard areas.

recommendations.summary: Distinguish required versus optional steps with trust-building copy.
recommendations.rationale: Users are more likely to continue when they know what can be deferred and what is reversible.
recommendations.expected_user_outcome: Admins feel safer completing billing and invite steps because the flow communicates consequences clearly.

assumptions: The dashboard shell and navigation already exist.
The checklist is a conceptual addition, not a new standalone product area.
open_questions: Should billing appear before teammate invites for every workspace type?
What trust copy is legally acceptable around trial conversion timing?

notes: The Markdown copy may expand on recommendation trade-offs, but it should preserve recommendation ids and priorities.

machine_readable.surface: Onboarding checklist embedded in the dashboard overview page

selected_template.label: Checklist plus supporting detail panel
selected_template.usage: Use a left-side ordered checklist and a right-side detail panel for the currently selected step.

layout_regions.label: Setup header
layout_regions.purpose: Clarify the setup goal and reinforce the primary next action.

layout_regions.label: Checklist column
layout_regions.purpose: Show ordered setup steps with status, optionality, and lightweight progress cues.

layout_regions.label: Detail panel
layout_regions.purpose: Explain what happens next before the admin commits to the selected step.

machine_readable.selection_rationale: The split-panel template keeps setup context visible while avoiding modal hopping and preserving a low-fidelity planning shape.
alternatives_considered: Single-card dashboard nudge without persistent checklist context
interaction_notes: Keep each checklist item scannable in one line.
Avoid hidden rules for when a step becomes complete.
communication_notes: The setup header should explain what the checklist is for and why it matters now.
The detail panel should answer what happens next before the admin commits to the selected step.
Billing-related actions should keep trust copy visible near the decision point.

microcopy_rewrites.guidance: Make the first required step and the benefit of starting now obvious.
microcopy_rewrites.example_text: Finish your workspace setup by starting the first required step below.

microcopy_rewrites.guidance: Clearly distinguish required steps from optional steps without sounding punitive.
State the action and imply what happens next.
Explain reversibility or defer-ability near trust-sensitive actions.
microcopy_rewrites.example_text: You can come back to optional steps later.

notes: Expose the grouped v1 outputs as named Markdown subsections under this artifact class.

flows.name: Admin completes the initial setup path
flows.goal: Reach an invited-and-billable workspace state without losing orientation.
entry_points: Fresh dashboard visit after workspace creation

steps.label: See the recommended first step
steps.intent: Orient the admin immediately with one primary action.
steps.user_decision: Decide whether to start the recommended setup task now.
steps.system_response: Show one clearly recommended next step, why it matters, and whether it is required.

steps.label: Review why the step matters
steps.intent: Set expectations before the admin commits.
steps.user_decision: Decide whether to proceed, defer, or learn more.
steps.system_response: Explain what the step changes, whether it is reversible, and what the next state will be.

steps.label: Complete the selected setup step
steps.intent: Move setup forward without losing place in the checklist.
steps.user_decision: Confirm the chosen action and complete the current step.
steps.system_response: Preserve orientation, show completion feedback, and surface the next recommended step.

steps.label: Return to the checklist with updated status
steps.intent: Preserve momentum into the next suggested action.
steps.system_response: Update progress status and keep the next required action easy to find.

states_covered: Checklist with an in-progress required step
Completed step with the next recommendation surfaced
edge_cases: Invite action fails because the teammate already has a pending invite

flows.name: Admin defers an optional step
flows.goal: Leave setup without feeling blocked or punished.
entry_points: Optional checklist item is opened from the dashboard

steps.label: Open an optional step
steps.intent: Understand what the optional action would do.
steps.user_decision: Decide whether the optional task is worth doing now.
steps.system_response: Clarify the benefit of the optional step without making it feel blocking.

steps.label: Defer the optional step
steps.intent: Allow the admin to continue without feeling blocked.
steps.user_decision: Choose to skip this step for now.
steps.system_response: Confirm that deferring is allowed and keep the next required action visible.

steps.label: Resume the checklist
steps.intent: Return attention to the next required action.
steps.system_response: Restore checklist context and avoid punishing the defer decision.

states_covered: Dashboard resumes with a lightweight reminder
handoff_notes: Optional steps should never obscure the primary required action.

cross_flow_notes: The checklist should preserve place when the admin leaves and returns later.

human_readable.section_heading: User Flow / Data Flow / Operation Flow / State Transitions
notes: Expose the grouped v1 outputs as named Markdown subsections under this artifact class.

machine_readable.export_target: Human designer or low-fidelity mock generator
machine_readable.prompt_text: Design a low-fidelity concept for a workspace onboarding checklist that lives inside an existing dashboard shell. Treat the experience as a conversation with a first-time admin: make the first action obvious, distinguish required and optional steps, and explain what happens next before each commitment. Keep the layout to a checklist column plus a supporting detail panel. Preserve trust-building copy near billing-related actions. Do not produce implementation-ready components, editable preview controls, or framework-specific instructions.
must_include: A split checklist and detail-panel layout
One primary next-step call to action
Clear labeling for required versus optional steps
handoff_notes: Annotated grayscale wireframe output is sufficient.

notes: The Markdown bundle may repeat the full prompt verbatim for easy copy/paste reuse.

states: After the first required step completes
read_only_constraints: The preview must be read-only and may rely on static placeholder content.
Do not expose editing controls, persistence, or live service integrations.
required_annotations: Label which checklist items are required versus optional.
Call out trust copy near billing-related actions.
non_goals: No editor contract for rearranging checklist items.
No implementation-ready API or component schema.
No live data wiring or runtime behavior promises.
visualization_notes: One main screen plus two annotated state variations is enough.
Static notes are preferred over interactive controls.
